data.title: VeChain VET, Rekord, and AMRC collaborate on EU Digital Product Passport system, handling over 100K events per month.
data.description: VeChain, Rekord, and Sheffield's AMRC have collaborated to introduce a production-scale DPP infrastructure in anticipation of EU mandates. The system is capable of handling over 100,000 events per month. For further details, click on the link provided.

data.title: Muhammad Ibrahim takes office as IICA Director General, vows to enhance agriculture cooperation in the Americas with support from 30+ countries.
data.description: In an event held in San Jose, Costa Rica on January 16, 2026, Muhammad Ibrahim officially took on the role of Director General at the Inter-American Institute for Cooperation on Agriculture (IICA). The inauguration ceremony was attended by key figures from over thirty nations, including ministers and senior officials.

data.title: Celonis aids Fujitsu in enhancing inventory management and enabling AI-driven transformation globally.
data.description: In Tokyo, Munich, and New York, Celonis has revealed that its platform is driving business innovation at Fujitsu and other companies worldwide.

data.title: Seela launches new AI creative platform Jan 16, 2026. Features text-to-image, image-to-image, and multi-model AI chatbot comparison.
data.description: On January 16, 2026, in Los Angeles, Seela introduced a new comprehensive AI-driven creative platform. This platform combines text-to-image, image-to-image, and multi-model AI chatbot comparison tools in one user-friendly interface. This integrated workspace allows users to efficiently explore, analyze, and produce content with improved precision.

data.title: VeChain's VET shines in CNBC program, VeBetter reaches 5.5M users with eco-friendly data verified on blockchain.
data.description: VeChain's sustainability platform was highlighted in a documentary partnered with CNBC. The ecosystem boasts an impressive 5.5 million users and provides verified environmental metrics through blockchain technology.

data.title: 2025 Annual Report by U.S. Commission on Social Status of Black Men and Boys focuses on impact of school-to-prison pipeline.
data.description: On January 16, 2026, the U.S. Commission on the Social Status of Black Men and Boys (CSSBMB) issued its 2025 Annual Report, which delves into the impact of exclusionary school discipline practices on educational equity and civil rights. The report emphasizes the persistent issue of how these practices hinder the progress of Black male students, with staggering statistics revealing the extent of this problem. The report sheds light on the urgent need for reforms in the education system to address the disparities faced by Black male students in the U.S.
